certificationGate.purpose: All strict conformance, parity, package-boundary, and evidence lanes must be green from RC artifacts before the repository may claim certification.
excludes: live support window on published artifacts

promotionGate.purpose: Only after certification is complete may the repository publish, tag, create the GitHub release, and run the live post-release support window on published artifacts.
includes: live support window on published artifacts

claimLanguage.externalMarkdownTarget: externally frozen CommonMark/GFM markdown target conformance for the declared profile set
claimLanguage.note: No broader standards language may be used unless the evidence actually supports it.

requiredBrowserTool.name: Playwright Test
requiredBrowserTool.purpose: canonical real-browser matrix, accessibility, and visual-regression execution engine for final closure lanes

checks.detail: Phase 16 explicitly splits certification closure from promotion/release execution.
Phase 16 explicitly freezes the final claim language to repository-internal certification plus the externally frozen CommonMark/GFM target only.
20/20 CommonMark corpus cases passed in the current subset lane.
7/7 default GFM corpus cases passed in the current subset lane.
8/8 optional profile cases passed in the current named-extension lane.
17/17 editor keyboard/command smoke checks remain green.
40/40 toolbar/selection structural checks remain green from the Phase 5 checkpoint artifact.
71/71 preview/export checks remain green, plus 5/5 current app preview/export checks.
Accessibility-adjacent evidence remains green across preview/export (5/5), shell parity (11/11), and i18n (23/23) artifacts.
The provided checkpoint container does not include a full Playwright/browser matrix. Existing structural smoke scripts remain available, but a true browser matrix lane cannot be marked green here.
Static CSS digests and HTML baselines are present, but there is no browser-captured pixel diff run in this environment.
Portable subset install succeeded, but the full app tarball install remains blocked by unavailable registry dependencies in this environment.
Manifest validation (4), compatibility (4), signed artifact verification (1), and runtime smoke (pass) were evaluated.
193/193 documentation/evidence checks remain green and current package boundary/export validations also passed.
Executed subset lanes are green, but the repository still does not run a full official end-to-end frozen CommonMark/GFM corpus and optional-profile closure across the entire declared boundary.
The living v1→v2 parity ledger no longer shows open user-visible P0 parity rows and the shell/settings/theme/i18n/package evidence checkpoint artifacts remain green.
Package boundary validation reported no violations.
Signed catalog artifacts validated successfully against the generated trust policy.
Phase 11 package documentation/evidence matrix recorded no failing workspace entries.
The repository still carries the Phase 13 RC validation evidence and tarball inventory as the basis for certification-from-RC artifacts.

blockers.detail: The provided checkpoint container does not include a full Playwright/browser matrix. Existing structural smoke scripts remain available, but a true browser matrix lane cannot be marked green here.
Static CSS digests and HTML baselines are present, but there is no browser-captured pixel diff run in this environment.
Portable subset install succeeded, but the full app tarball install remains blocked by unavailable registry dependencies in this environment.
Executed subset lanes are green, but the repository still does not run a full official end-to-end frozen CommonMark/GFM corpus and optional-profile closure across the entire declared boundary.

checks.detail: Promotion remains blocked because no publish step has been executed from the validated RC artifacts in this environment.
No published npm artifacts exist for this checkpoint environment. The strongest available evidence remains the RC tarball validation recorded in Phase 13.
Git tag creation was not executed because the checkpoint zip does not include a Git repository and release blockers remain.
GitHub release creation was not executed because the checkpoint environment has no repository remote/auth context and release blockers remain.
No public published release exists in this environment, so the support window is policy-defined for the prepared bundle rather than live on published npm artifacts.
Patch-only rule is declared for the train.
